content:
# Final Review Votes
**Producer:** agent:claude/claude-opus-4-7-max

## Summary

Verified both amendments against the actual code. Amendment fr-4-76d8edde: confirmed backward compatibility break - master's validate() did not require bead description/bead_type/priority/labels, and master's canonicalize_bundle() backfilled empty covered_by from derived coverage (removed in this branch at bundle.rs:1155), so bundles accepted and persisted under master will fail the new stricter validate() when re-read during milestone handoff at service.rs:2358. Amendment fr-4-0182f58f: confirmed analyze_workstream_order() at bundle.rs:763,766 silently discards both unresolved dependency references and intra-workstream edges, so infer_workstream_order() returns a non-empty order for any single-workstream bead cycle (e.g., A -> B -> A) and for workstreams that reference nonexistent beads. Existing test proves the empty-vec convention holds for cross-workstream cycles, so the asymmetry is a real footgun. Both are genuine defects with reasonable remediations.

## Votes

- `fr-4-76d8edde`: Accept
  Verified. In master, validate() did not require bead description/bead_type/priority/labels, and canonicalize_bundle() backfilled empty acceptance_map[i].covered_by from derived bead.acceptance_criteria before the declared.is_empty() check. This branch adds hard requirements for those bead fields and a pre-canonicalization check at bundle.rs:279 while removing the backfill. Since generate_and_commit_milestone_bundle persists the raw planner payload at service.rs:1829 and extract_milestone_bundle_handoff revalidates via bundle.validate() at service.rs:2358, any completed requirements run whose stored bundle was accepted under master's looser rules will now fail handoff/materialization after upgrade. The project prompt also explicitly declares the work as ADDITIVE, so breaking previously-accepted payloads is out of character. Remediation (backfill covered_by on canonicalize or add a migration/loosen legacy validation) is reasonable and preserves the stricter planner prompt guidance.
- `fr-4-0182f58f`: Accept
  Verified at bundle.rs:742-799. analyze_workstream_order() only adds a workstream-level edge when the dependency alias resolves via bead_index.get(&alias) (line 763) and when upstream != downstream (line 766). Therefore (a) a dependency referencing a nonexistent bead is silently dropped, and (b) a same-workstream cycle like A -> B -> A produces zero workstream edges, leaving indegree all zero, so infer_workstream_order() returns a non-empty order at bundle.rs:1006-1013 despite the underlying bead graph being unsortable. This is inconsistent with the existing test infer_workstream_order_returns_empty_when_workstream_dependencies_interleave, which establishes that empty-vec signals an unsortable graph. Future callers relying on that convention will be misled. Validating the bead-level graph via dependency_graph_analysis_from_nodes before collapsing to workstreams (or returning Result) is a small, targeted fix that aligns the function's behavior with its existing contract.
